expectations.text: Starts with a failing test for the new feature before writing any implementation
expectations.evidence: The response starts with `test_save10_applies_10_percent_discount()` and explicitly says to run it first with an expected RED (`AttributeError`) before any implementation is added.

expectations.text: Each new test is followed by implementation — not all tests first
expectations.evidence: SAVE10 and SAVE20 tests are each followed by implementation, but invalid-code rejection is already implemented in Slice 1 (`else: raise ValueError('Invalid discount code')`) before the invalid-code test is introduced in Slice 3. The response also notes this test may already be GREEN, so strict test-then-new-implementation sequencing is not met for every new test.

expectations.text: Tests go through the public ShoppingCart interface (not internal _items directly)
expectations.evidence: All tests interact through `ShoppingCart()`, `add_item(...)`, `apply_discount_code(...)`, and `total()`. No test reads or mutates `_items` directly.

expectations.text: The discount feature is built incrementally — later tests build on earlier ones
expectations.evidence: The flow is incremental: first SAVE10 behavior, then SAVE20 behavior, then an explicit invalid-code test, followed by a post-green refactor to `DISCOUNT_CODES`.

expectations.text: At least one edge case is tested (invalid discount code or empty cart)
expectations.evidence: The response includes `test_invalid_discount_code_is_rejected()` and checks `pytest.raises(ValueError, match='Invalid discount code')` for an unsupported code.

uncertainties: The grading is based on response/transcript content only; no recorded pytest execution output is available in this run directory.
The API shape (`apply_discount_code(code)` mutating state consumed by `total()`) is inferred from the solution and is reasonable for the prompt.
needs_review: Assertion 2 is strict: one introduced test (invalid discount code) is not followed by new implementation because the behavior was already present earlier.
workarounds: Used response/transcript evidence and retained zero-valued telemetry fields to match prior local grading schema when runtime telemetry is unavailable.

suggestions: To fully satisfy strict TDD sequencing for this rubric, introduce the invalid-code test before adding invalid-code handling in implementation.
eval_feedback.overall: The run demonstrates clear incremental TDD guidance and public-interface tests, with one rubric miss on strict test-then-implementation ordering for every introduced test.
